steps.title: Place the opening example
steps.content: The Grenada detail opens the passage by giving an example of a shift away from solid fuel use: solid fuels fell by more than a third as a share of household fuel between 2000 and 2019. The text then says such shifts are often explained by the energy ladder, a model focused mainly on income, before showing through the Kenya study that this model is reductive.

steps.title: Match the passage structure
steps.content: Choice A says the change is poorly suited for evaluating the energy ladder, but the passage uses Grenada as a normal example of the kind of shift being discussed. Choice B invents two equally compelling explanations. Choice D claims Grenada and Kenya have a similar cause, which the passage does not say. Grenada illustrates a phenomenon often, but inadequately, accounted for by the energy ladder. The answer is C.
